creator: wollman@xyz.csail.mit.edu
text:
After upgrading to openssh-portable-6.3.p1,1, GSSAPI key exchange
fails.  The client receives a disconnect like so:

OpenSSH_6.2p2-hpn13v14 FreeBSD-openssh-portable-6.2.p2_3,1, OpenSSL 0.9.8y 5 Feb 2013

debug1: /usr/local/etc/ssh/ssh_config line 56: Applying options for *

debug1: REQUESTED ENC.NAME is 'aes128-ctr'
debug1: kex: server->client aes128-ctr hmac-md5-etm@openssh.com none
debug2: mac_setup: found hmac-md5-etm@openssh.com
debug1: REQUESTED ENC.NAME is 'aes128-ctr'
debug1: kex: client->server aes128-ctr hmac-md5-etm@openssh.com none
debug1: Doing group exchange

Connection closed by 128.31.0.28

The server log shows:

Oct 15 15:46:31 xyz sshd[89626]: SSH: Server;Ltype: Version;Remote: 128.30.28.20-55528;Protocol: 2.0;Client: OpenSSH_6.2p2-hpn13v14 FreeBSD-openssh-portable-6.2.p2_3,1
Oct 15 15:46:31 xyz sshd[89626]: SSH: Server;Ltype: Kex;Remote: 128.30.28.20-55528;Enc: aes128-ctr;MAC: hmac-md5-etm@openssh.com;Comp: none [preauth]
Oct 15 15:46:31 xyz sshd[89626]: fatal: unsupported kex alg gss-gex-sha1-toWM5Slw5Ew8Mqkay+al2g== [preauth]

An algorithm proposed by the server should not be failing.

Fix: 

Don't know.  Looking at the source, this seems to be a "can't happen"
condition.  I've downgraded to the last 6.2 for now.
How-To-Repeat: 
Upgrade to 6.3 and try to log in to a host that uses GSSAPI Key Exchange.
